cards.back: A duopoly model where one firm is the leader (Stackelberg firm) and the other firm is the follower (Cobb-Douglas firm)
The firm that makes the strategic decision first, setting the market price and production level, and then the follower firm adjusts to this decision
The firm that responds to the Stackelberg firm's decision, setting its own production level and cost function
The point at which the Stackelberg firm and the Cobb-Douglas firm produce at the same level, resulting in no change in the market price
The Stackelberg firm maximizes profit by choosing the optimal price and production level, while the Cobb-Douglas firm adjusts to this decision to maximize its own profit
